frameworks.name: mixture-of-agents reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Static MF/MB mixture extended with hidden Markov state to capture within-session strategy shifts.

frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: MF agent contrasted with MB as one expert in the mixture.

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: MB control dominates early exploration and later exploitation phases.

frameworks.name: hidden Markov model
frameworks.one_line: Latent state dynamics governing temporal shifts in agent weighting.

core_question: Can a hidden-state mixture-of-agents model capture within-session shifts in reinforcement-learning strategy?
key_assumptions: behavior arises from competing MF and MB experts
agent contributions can change discretely over time via latent states
OFC encoding reflects the active strategy state
key_findings: within-session progression: MB exploration to MB exploitation to disengagement
inferred hidden states predict response-time changes
inferred states predict shifts in OFC neural encoding
interpretations: strategy is non-stationary and dynamic within a session, not a fixed mixture
OFC encoding tracks latent strategy state rather than a single computation
